sourceBoundary.privacyBoundary: Do not commit or depend on private raw Marketing Event artifacts, raw API responses, tenant IDs, app IDs, list IDs, screenshots, or package payloads.
sourceBoundary.businessDataBoundary: Do not copy Marketing Event-specific field names, labels, sample data, or business semantics into unrelated apps. Map the structure to the current App Plan, Page Function Plan, and Application Design System.

goldenReferences.purpose: Default polished operations, portfolio, request, service, project, vendor, contract, facility, pipeline, and work-queue dashboard construction style.
defaultFor: service desk or work queue dashboard
mustNotUseFor: dashboard whose Page Function Plan explicitly requires a different plugin-contained pattern
generationContract: Start generated dashboard resources from the current App Plan, Page Function Plan, Application Design System, and verified Yeeflow UI hard gates.
Use this golden reference as the default dashboard construction style unless the page function clearly requires another plugin-contained pattern.
Adapt page title, subtitle, filters, metrics, source lists, fields, sorting, grouping, labels, actions, and mobile behavior to the current app.
Do not copy Marketing Event-specific field names or sample values unless the current app is actually about marketing events.
Keep Page Function Plan business-only; this registry is consumed by dashboard design, blueprint, and resource generation after PFP approval.

references.purpose: Default Dashboard page shell with a top-level Main container and nested Content container.
requiredStructure: Main container is the page root dashboard body.
Content container is nested directly inside Main.
All visible dashboard sections are nested inside Content.
Root dashboard padding remains governed by existing dashboard root padding hard gates.
Main and Content use meaningful navigator labels.
references.fallback: Use another plugin-contained dashboard shell only when selected by downstream dashboard pattern rules and documented in the blueprint.

references.purpose: Dashboard header band for title, subtitle, page description, filters, and page-level actions.
requiredContent: page-level filter/action row when required by the Page Function Plan
primary and secondary actions such as create, export, open detail, or navigation when required by the Page Function Plan
requiredBindings: actions must map to App Plan resources and plugin-known Container/Button action mappings
filter row must use source lists and fields from the App Plan
references.fallback: If actions cannot be safely generated, render non-interactive status/help text and record the deferred action boundary.

references.purpose: Dashboard filter group with filter icon area, multiple filter wrappers, and real data-bound filters.
requiredContent: multiple filters when the Page Function Plan requires them
filter variables consumed by KPI, Collection, Data table, Kanban, or other target regions
forbidden: static Text controls pretending to be filters
Marketing Event-specific filter names in unrelated apps
references.fallback: If an exact filter control is not export-proven for the required field type, mark the filter export-learning-required and keep business filter intent traceable.

references.purpose: KPI/Summary metric card area with hidden runtime Summary sources where required and polished visible KPI cards.
requiredContent: hidden/runtime Summary data source host when Yeeflow Summary binding requires it
requiredBindings: Summary/KPI binding or explicit fallback boundary
styleIntent: value hierarchy distinct from label/helper text
no static numeric KPI values unless explicitly approved as fallback
references.fallback: Use labeled fallback KPI values only when dynamic Summary binding is deferred and documented.

references.purpose: Major dashboard content section for a primary portfolio, pipeline, work queue, or operational data region.
requiredContent: section-level filters or view controls when required
section-level actions such as export, view, or status actions when required
references.fallback: Use a simpler plugin-contained dashboard section only when the Page Function Plan does not require a portfolio/work-queue region.

references.purpose: Grid-table style Collection data region with custom header grid and Collection-bound item row grid.
requiredContent: field columns mapped to the current App Plan
badge/status/health cells where status-like fields exist
progress or metric display cells where progress/rate fields exist
owner/person display where owner/person fields exist
row action or dynamic control cells when required
forbidden: plain scaffold tables when grid-table Collection is required
static copied Marketing Event fields in unrelated apps
unbound row buttons or action-looking controls
references.fallback: Use Data table only when the Page Function Plan explicitly requires a native table and the blueprint records why grid-table Collection is not appropriate.
